L2.description: A race condition vulnerability exists in the OAuth2 login flow where concurrent HTTP requests trigger unsynchronized access to a shared in-memory state map used for tracking login sessions. This allows remote unauthenticated attackers to cause a runtime panic and process termination, resulting in denial of service.

L3.area: OAuth2 authentication handler and its per-login state tracking logic
L3.description: A race condition vulnerability exists in the OAuth2 login flow where concurrent requests to the OAuth2 login and callback endpoints trigger unsynchronized read and write access to a shared in-memory map that tracks per-login state entries. This causes a Go runtime panic due to concurrent map operations, allowing remote unauthenticated attackers to crash the service when OAuth2 authentication is enabled.

ground_truth.reason: Concurrent HTTP requests to OAuth2 login and callback endpoints access a shared in-memory map without synchronization, causing a Go runtime panic (fatal error: concurrent map writes) and process termination.
